---
title: "Slide"
format: html
---

# Residual Autofluorescence Contamination in Single-Color Signatures

- Additionally, ability to profile signatures led to additional caveat to best practices for unmixing controls

- Where fluorophore is dim, and the antigen density low, even when gating brightest cells, signatures still end up with substantial autofluorescence contamination in their final signatures. 

- When pinpointing for 29 - 40 color panels, switching these controls for beads substantially improved unmixing.
